question.text: When an option buyer purchases a call option, the premium paid is recorded as:

answer.text: Debit: Option Premium Paid Account (Asset); Credit: Bank/Broker Account
Debit: Expense Account; Credit: Bank Account (immediately expensed)
Debit: Investment Account; Credit: Bank Account
No entry until the option is exercised

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The premium paid for an option is initially recorded as an asset (Option Premium Paid Account). It represents the right acquired. The entry is: Debit: Option Premium Paid Account (Asset) Credit: Bank/Broker Account
On exercise or expiry, this asset is either converted to profit/loss or written off.
**The Trap:** Premium is not immediately expensed - it is an asset until exercise/expiry. It is not an investment account in the traditional sense.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Option accounting differs from futures accounting (no daily MTM for buyers).
**Key Takeaway:** Option premium paid = asset initially. Debit Premium Account, Credit Bank. Written off on expiry if OTM.
